rationale.R1.1: Names the load-bearing concept (the contribution of DBChat) and shows where the draft drifts from it by discussing automatic schema documentation, which is not the original thesis.
rationale.R1.2: Identifies one consistency issue with a localized fix (the causal link between cost filtering and readability).
rationale.RX.1: Flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (the 68% agreement number without definition of what agreement means, inter-annotator baseline, or comparison to simpler doc-mining methods).
rationale.RX.3: Mostly within scope with one drift (discussion of automatic schema documentation).
rationale.RX.4: Output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content (the causal link between cost filtering and readability).
